Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_designation Status=None |Notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The dimensions, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == Artifacts == Artifacts were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the artifacts are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transformed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Information == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_designation Status=None |Notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The dimensions, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the artifacts are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transformed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Information == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The dimensions, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transformed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Information == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite method|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The dimensions, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transformed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Information == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite method|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transformed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Information == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite method|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta (Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite method|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite method|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}} |burial goods= |other_designation Status=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会}}, the chamber measured [[Category:pp error]]approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Buried Figure == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群}} The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward -
