Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}} |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|qq=}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|qq=}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meisho Zue|qq=}}, the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Gate|qq=}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Mirrors|qq=}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]

revision.comment: 48 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市) up to rev 93539215
revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2014年9月10日 (水) 05:39 (UTC)}} {{otheruses2|名古屋市熱田区にある古墳|名古屋市守山区にある古墳|白鳥古墳群|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
拝所 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[熱田区]][[白鳥 (名古屋市)|白鳥]]1丁目2番 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=白鳥古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長70[[メートル|m]]
高さ7m |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （伝）[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]
（推定）[[尾張氏]]首長 |出土品 = |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|全景]] '''白鳥古墳'''（しろとりこふん / しらとりこふん）は、[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[熱田区]][[白鳥 (名古屋市)|白鳥]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[熱田神宮]]では「白鳥御陵」と称する{{Sfn|宮記|2012|pp=32-35}}。 == 概要 == [[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたと考えられている[[前方後円墳]]で、[[法持寺]]のすぐ隣に位置、[[白鳥公園]]に隣接する。かつては法持寺が管理していたが、[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）からは[[熱田神宮]]に、戦後は名古屋市に管理が移された。 墳丘には[[須恵器|須恵質]]の[[円筒埴輪]]が巡らされていた。また、かつては墳丘の東側から北西側にかけて幅10メートルほどの周濠があったと推定されるが、現在では確認できない状態となっている。 [[天保]]8年（[[1837年]]）8月14日の台風の際に陵上の樹が倒れて内部の石室が露出。『[[尾張名所図会]]』附録2巻によれば、石室は全長約3.7メートル、全幅1.2-1.5メートル、深さ1.5-1.8メートルの石垣組みで、5枚の蓋石によって覆われていたという。 == 墳丘 == * 墳丘長：70[[メートル]] * 後円部 ** 直径：45メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：30メートル ** 幅：55メートル ** 高さ：7メートル 墳丘長70メートルなどとされるが、これらは[[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）に[[名古屋大学]]が調査測量を行った際の数値である。しかし前方部南端は道路建設で、後円部の東側は[[鳥居]]の建設などで、また西側も法持寺の移転改築などに伴って墳裾部が掘削されており、元々の形を留めていない。前述の調査で前方部が削平されている可能性が指摘されているほか、墳丘東側に幅5メートルほどの平坦面があった事から、2段築成であった可能性もあるとされる。 == 出土品 == 法持寺の僧侶の手によって一旦取り出されたが、寺社官への言上・評議の結果、石室へ戻され、墳丘も旧状へと復したと伝わっている。その際に形状や数などが記録された。 * 装身具 ** [[勾玉]] ** 切子玉 ** 管玉 ** 双魚形腰佩 * [[銅鏡]] ** 六鈴鏡 * [[馬具]] ** 楕円形鏡板 ** f字形鏡板 ** 劍菱形杏葉 ** 鐘形杏葉 ** 辻金具 * [[武器]] ** [[直刀]] ** 鉾 ** 小刀 * [[須恵器]] - 器台付三連坩、子坩四個脚付短頸壷、蓋付小坩、高坏、蓋類、器台 出土遺物の書写図としては次の3件が伝わる{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}}。 * 小田切春江書写図 *: 墨絵。『尾張名所図会附録』に収録されている。石槨の図に「横四五尺竪二間餘深サ五六尺」と記載されている。 * 植松茂岳書写図 *: 墨絵。植松茂岳の書写した原本を渡辺政香が再写した『尾張国白鳥山旧陵中器物略図』は[[西尾市岩瀬文庫]]が所蔵している。奥書に天保九年に写したと記述されている。石函図に「長弐間半、巾三尺余、深四尺」と記載されている。『植松茂岳_第2部』に翻刻されている。 * 熱田白鳥山法持寺史 書写図 *: 彩色されている。『熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版』に原本の写真が掲載されており、末尾のページには「白鳥山法持寺　石雄代」との記載がされている。原本は末尾部分が紛失しており{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=245}}、「大府市誌」に末尾部分を含めた写真があり、そこから収録している。石室内の配置図が記載されている。『「尾張の遺跡と遺物」第二十七号』に転写され、『尾張の遺跡と遺物_中巻[復刻版]』で復刻再刊されている。『白鳥古墳第2次発掘調査報告書』と『熱田地陵墓考』にも転写されている。 == 被葬者 == 考古学的には、被葬者は[[尾張氏]]首長と推定される。 熱田神宮社伝では、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵としている。これは、能褒野に葬られてのち白鳥となった日本武尊が当地に降り立ったという伝承に基づく。神宮では、北方約300メートルにある[[断夫山古墳]]を「陀武夫御墓」と称して日本武尊妃の[[宮簀媛]]（みやずひめ）の墓とし、現在も毎年5月8日に白鳥古墳と断夫山古墳とにおいて御陵墓祭を行なっている{{Sfn|宮記|2012|pp=32-35}}。 == その他 == [[本居宣長]]は当地を訪れた際に、「しきしまの やまとこひしみ 白とりの かけりいましし あとところこれ」と歌を詠んだといわれ{{Sfn|宮記|2012|pp=32-35}}、その歌碑が公園の堀川側の入口横に建立されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（名古屋市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|title=熱田・瑞穂区の考古遺跡|series=文化財叢書 第八一号|author=三渡俊一郎|year=1981}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=熱田区・白鳥古墳 -法持寺改築に伴う発掘調査の概要-|publisher=名古屋市教育委員会|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 23 愛知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=陵墓|title=熱田神宮宮記|publisher=[[熱田神宮]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122745 白鳥古墳第II次発掘調査報告書（名古屋市文化財調査報告21）]|publisher=名古屋市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[断夫山古墳]] * [[熱田神宮]] * [[白鳥陵]] * [[白鳥古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しろとりこふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:熱田区の歴史]] [[Category:尾張氏|墓しろとりこふん]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓しろとりこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] to [[Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{参照方法|date=2014年9月10日 (水) 05:39 (UTC)}} {{otheruses2|名古屋市熱田区にある古墳|名古屋市守山区にある古墳|白鳥古墳群|白鳥塚古墳 (名古屋市)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 白鳥古墳 |画像 = [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
拝所 |所在地 = [[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[熱田区]][[白鳥 (名古屋市)|白鳥]]1丁目2番 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=白鳥古墳}} |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長70[[メートル|m]]
高さ7m |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]初頭 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （伝）[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]
（推定）[[尾張氏]]首長 |出土品 = |史跡指定 = なし |特記事項 = }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|全景]] '''白鳥古墳'''（しろとりこふん / しらとりこふん）は、[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[熱田区]][[白鳥 (名古屋市)|白鳥]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[熱田神宮]]では「白鳥御陵」と称する{{Sfn|宮記|2012|pp=32-35}}。 == 概要 == [[6世紀]]初頭に築造されたと考えられている[[前方後円墳]]で、[[法持寺]]のすぐ隣に位置、[[白鳥公園]]に隣接する。かつては法持寺が管理していたが、[[1876年]]（[[明治]]9年）からは[[熱田神宮]]に、戦後は名古屋市に管理が移された。 墳丘には[[須恵器|須恵質]]の[[円筒埴輪]]が巡らされていた。また、かつては墳丘の東側から北西側にかけて幅10メートルほどの周濠があったと推定されるが、現在では確認できない状態となっている。 [[天保]]8年（[[1837年]]）8月14日の台風の際に陵上の樹が倒れて内部の石室が露出。『[[尾張名所図会]]』附録2巻によれば、石室は全長約3.7メートル、全幅1.2-1.5メートル、深さ1.5-1.8メートルの石垣組みで、5枚の蓋石によって覆われていたという。 == 墳丘 == * 墳丘長：70[[メートル]] * 後円部 ** 直径：45メートル ** 高さ：6.5メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：30メートル ** 幅：55メートル ** 高さ：7メートル 墳丘長70メートルなどとされるが、これらは[[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）に[[名古屋大学]]が調査測量を行った際の数値である。しかし前方部南端は道路建設で、後円部の東側は[[鳥居]]の建設などで、また西側も法持寺の移転改築などに伴って墳裾部が掘削されており、元々の形を留めていない。前述の調査で前方部が削平されている可能性が指摘されているほか、墳丘東側に幅5メートルほどの平坦面があった事から、2段築成であった可能性もあるとされる。 == 出土品 == 法持寺の僧侶の手によって一旦取り出されたが、寺社官への言上・評議の結果、石室へ戻され、墳丘も旧状へと復したと伝わっている。その際に形状や数などが記録された。 * 装身具 ** [[勾玉]] ** 切子玉 ** 管玉 ** 双魚形腰佩 * [[銅鏡]] ** 六鈴鏡 * [[馬具]] ** 楕円形鏡板 ** f字形鏡板 ** 劍菱形杏葉 ** 鐘形杏葉 ** 辻金具 * [[武器]] ** [[直刀]] ** 鉾 ** 小刀 * [[須恵器]] - 器台付三連坩、子坩四個脚付短頸壷、蓋付小坩、高坏、蓋類、器台 出土遺物の書写図としては次の3件が伝わる{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}}。 * 小田切春江書写図 *: 墨絵。『尾張名所図会附録』に収録されている。石槨の図に「横四五尺竪二間餘深サ五六尺」と記載されている。 * 植松茂岳書写図 *: 墨絵。植松茂岳の書写した原本を渡辺政香が再写した『尾張国白鳥山旧陵中器物略図』は[[西尾市岩瀬文庫]]が所蔵している。奥書に天保九年に写したと記述されている。石函図に「長弐間半、巾三尺余、深四尺」と記載されている。『植松茂岳_第2部』に翻刻されている。 * 熱田白鳥山法持寺史 書写図 *: 彩色されている。『熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版』に原本の写真が掲載されており、末尾のページには「白鳥山法持寺　石雄代」との記載がされている。原本は末尾部分が紛失しており{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=245}}、「大府市誌」に末尾部分を含めた写真があり、そこから収録している。石室内の配置図が記載されている。『「尾張の遺跡と遺物」第二十七号』に転写され、『尾張の遺跡と遺物_中巻[復刻版]』で復刻再刊されている。『白鳥古墳第2次発掘調査報告書』と『熱田地陵墓考』にも転写されている。 == 被葬者 == 考古学的には、被葬者は[[尾張氏]]首長と推定される。 熱田神宮社伝では、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の陵としている。これは、能褒野に葬られてのち白鳥となった日本武尊が当地に降り立ったという伝承に基づく。神宮では、北方約300メートルにある[[断夫山古墳]]を「陀武夫御墓」と称して日本武尊妃の[[宮簀媛]]（みやずひめ）の墓とし、現在も毎年5月8日に白鳥古墳と断夫山古墳とにおいて御陵墓祭を行なっている{{Sfn|宮記|2012|pp=32-35}}。 == その他 == [[本居宣長]]は当地を訪れた際に、「しきしまの やまとこひしみ 白とりの かけりいましし あとところこれ」と歌を詠んだといわれ{{Sfn|宮記|2012|pp=32-35}}、その歌碑が公園の堀川側の入口横に建立されている。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（名古屋市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|title=熱田・瑞穂区の考古遺跡|series=文化財叢書 第八一号|author=三渡俊一郎|year=1981}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=熱田区・白鳥古墳 -法持寺改築に伴う発掘調査の概要-|publisher=名古屋市教育委員会|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=白鳥古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 23 愛知県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|白鳥古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=陵墓|title=熱田神宮宮記|publisher=[[熱田神宮]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/122745 白鳥古墳第II次発掘調査報告書（名古屋市文化財調査報告21）]|publisher=名古屋市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[断夫山古墳]] * [[熱田神宮]] * [[白鳥陵]] * [[白鳥古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しろとりこふん}} [[Category:名古屋市の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:熱田区の歴史]] [[Category:尾張氏|墓しろとりこふん]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|墓しろとりこふん]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}} |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|qq=}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|qq=}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meisho Zue|qq=}}, the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Gate|qq=}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Mirrors|qq=}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}}

(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}} |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|qq=}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|qq=}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meisho Zue|qq=}}, the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Gate|qq=}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Mirrors|qq=}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Gate|qq=}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Mirrors|qq=}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}} |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|qq=}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|qq=}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meisho Zue|qq=}}, the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Gate|qq=}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Mirrors|qq=}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}}

According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}} |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|qq=}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|qq=}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari Meisho Zue|ja|尾張名所図会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Meisho Zue|qq=}}, the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii Gate|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii Gate|qq=}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze Mirrors|ja|銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze Mirrors|qq=}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue Ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue Ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari Clan|ja|尾張氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Clan|qq=}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|ja|断夫山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danpusan Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|ja|熱田神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atsuta Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Yamato Takeru Legend
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35|07|38|N|136|54|12|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiratori Kofun}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|qq=}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|qq=}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|qq=}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta Ward]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]][[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]]}} [[sn:Shiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] [[sn:hiratori Kofun (Nagoya)]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]
