But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11580543}}

The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11580543}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580543]] Wikidata name: Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11580543}}

Worship area |location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11580543}}

These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580543]] Wikidata name: Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{draft categories| }} [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location=[[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Nagoya|Nagoya]], [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]] |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]], it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

According to the a11endix of the [[:en:Owari meisho zue|Owari Meisho Zue]], the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a [[:en:Torii|Torii Gate]], and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]] ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze Mirrors]]: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the [[:en:Owari clan|Owari Clan]]. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]], located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * [[:en:Danpusan Kofun|Danpusan Kofun]] * [[:en:Atsuta Shrine|Atsuta Shrine]] * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)]][[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11580543]] Wikidata name: Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 10, 2014}} {{otheruses2|Kofun located in Atsuta Ward, Nagoya|Kofun in Moriyama Ward, Nagoya|Shiratori Kofun Group|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Nagoya)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Shiratori Kofun |image=[[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) haisho.JPG|250px]]
Worship area |location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|WD=Q11751|lt=Nagoya}}, [[Atsuta Ward]], {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}} 1-2 |coordinates={{Coord|35.127222|N|136.903333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun|Keyhole-shaped]] |area=Kofun length: 70 meters
Height: 7 meters |built=Early 6th century |burial=Unknown |Buried figure=(Traditionally) {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
(Archaeologically) Head of the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari Clan}} |burial goods= |designation1=None |notes= |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} [[File:Shiratori-kofun (Nagoya) zenkei.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Overall view]] '''Shiratori Kofun''' (白鳥古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirotori, Nagoya|ja|白鳥 (名古屋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirotori, Nagoya|1=Shirotori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56433291}}, Atsuta Ward, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped. At {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}}, it is referred to as the "Shiratori no Misasagi" (白鳥御陵).{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Overview == The Shiratori Kofun is believed to have been constructed in the early 6th century. It is located next to {{ill|Hōjiji Temple|ja|法持寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjiji Temple|1=Hōji-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11555169}} and adjacent to {{ill|Shiratori Park|ja|白鳥公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Park|1=Shirotori Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17228816}}. Originally managed by Hōjiji Temple, control of the kofun was transferred to Atsuta Shrine in 1876 and later to Nagoya City after World War II. Cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} of Sue pottery quality once encircled the tumulus. Additionally, it is estimated that there was a moat approximately 10 meters wide extending from the east to the northwest side of the tumulus, but this is no longer visible today. During a typhoon on August 14, 1837 (Tenpō 8), a tree on the mound fell, exposing the internal stone chamber. According to the a11endix of the {{ill|Owari meisho zue|en|Owari meisho zue|WD=Q11465290|lt=Owari Meisho Zue}}, the chamber measured approximately 3.7 meters in total length, 1.2 to 1.5 meters in width, and 1.5 to 1.8 meters in depth. It was constructed with stone masonry and covered by five capstones. == Kofun area == * Kofun length: 70 meters * Rear Circular Mound: ''' Diameter: 45 meters ''' Height: 6.5 meters * Front Rectangular Portion: ''' Length: 30 meters ''' Width: 55 meters ''' Height: 7 meters The area, such as the 70-meter tumulus length, are based on a survey conducted by Nagoya University in 1951 (Shōwa 26). However, parts of the tumulus have been altered over time, including the south end of the front rectangular portion due to road construction, the east side of the rear circular mound for the construction of a {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii Gate}}, and the western side due to the relocation and reconstruction of Hōjiji Temple. These changes mean the original type is no longer intact. The 1951 survey suggested that the front portion may have been leveled and that the eastern side of the tumulus had a flat surface about 5 meters wide, possibly indicating a two-tiered construction. == burial goods == burial goods were once removed by the monks of Hōjiji Temple but later returned to the stone chamber following discussions with temple and shrine authorities. Records of their types and quantities were made at the time. * Personal Ornaments: ''' {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|WD=Q334232|lt=Magatama}} ''' Cut beads ''' Pipe-shaped beads ''' Twin-fish-shaped pendants * {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze Mirrors}}: ''' Six-bell mirrors * Horse Gear: ''' Oval-shaped mirrored plates ''' F-shaped mirrored plates ''' Diamond-shaped pendants ''' Bell-shaped pendants ''' Bridles * Weapons: ''' Straight swords ''' Spears ''' Daggers * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}: ''' Three-linked crucibles on stands ''' Four small crucibles ''' Lidded short-neck jars on footed stands ''' Bowls with lids ''' Containers and stands Three illustrations of the burial goods are known to exist:{{Sfn|熱田白鳥山法持寺史　法持寺蔵版|2012|p=244-245}} 1. Ink illustrations by Harue Odagiri, included in the a11endix of the Owari Meisho Zue. 2. Ink illustrations by Shigaku Uematsu, with a copy held by the Iwase Bunko Library in Nishio City. 3. A colored illustration featured in the Hōjiji Temple history book, which also provides a detailed map of the artifact placement within the stone chamber. == Interred_individual == Archaeological evidence suggests the buried figure is a head of the {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|WD=Q11465311|lt=Owari Clan}}. According to Atsuta Shrine lore, this tumulus is the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The legend states that after his burial at Nobono, Yamato Takeru transtypeed into a white bird and landed at this site. The shrine identifies the nearby {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun}}, located about 300 meters to the north, as the tomb of his consort, Princess Miyazu (宮簀媛). Every May 8, Atsuta Shrine conducts tomb rites at both Shiratori Kofun and Danpusan Kofun.{{Sfn|宮記|2012|11=32-35}} == Additional Intypeation == During his visit to the site, {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga}} composed a waka poem, which is inscribed on a stone monument near the park entrance by the Horikawa River: *"Shikishima no Yamato koishimi Shiratori no kakeri imashishi ato tokoro kore."* == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Historical explanation board (Nagoya City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Overview of Shiratori Kofun in Atsuta Ward - Hōjiji Temple Reconstruction|author=Nagoya City Board of Education|year=1986}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Shiratori Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 23 Aichi Prefecture names|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49023-9|ref={{Harvid|Shiratori Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=Tombs|title=The Chronicle of Atsuta Shrine|publisher=[[Atsuta Shrine]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|宮記|2012}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} * {{ill|Danpusan Kofun|en|Danpusan Kofun|WD=Q5221001|lt=Danpusan Kofun}} * {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|WD=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine}} * {{ill|Shiratori Ryo|ja|白鳥陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Ryo|qq=}} * {{ill|Shiratori Kofun Group|ja|白鳥古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Kofun Group|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)]] [[ja:白鳥古墳]] {{translated page|ja|白鳥古墳 (名古屋市)|version=93539215|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Shirotori-kofun (Nagoya, Aichi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11580543}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagoya]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
